notes_for_consumers: Brand-baseline creative posture + the Year-End retail moment, resolving for any BC that has no authored lane override (e.g. Northeast, Southeast, Midwest, West). The brand baseline applies universally; the Year-End block specifies moment-level emphasis. Where a lane override exists (Great Lakes, Southwest) it adds regional specifics on top of this. Compose: lane content (if any) over the Year-End emphasis applied through the baseline posture.

brand_baseline.posture: vehicle-as-hero, motion-forward, cinematic, capability-led. Every frame advances the spot. No isolated beats. The truck is the protagonist; the environment is the supporting actor.
principles: In-your-face creative matching the intensity of 'Nothing Stops Ram'
Vehicle-as-hero - truck in frame, in motion, in command
No people in frame for the Year-End retail demo; audience truth feeds environment and object choice, not casting
Capability-led - features and capabilities are the through-line; supers carry the claim
Frame-to-frame continuity - the prior cell's resolution informs the next
Quality reclamation visible - lead with JD Power VDS on quality-adjacent moments

year_end_moment.framing: Sales-event creative, not brand-build. Wrap Up The Year Sales Event sits in Q4 downstream of the Football launch (Aug 27) and brand campaign launch (Jan 20). Inherits the established NSR voice rather than building it.
year_end_moment.dial_posture: High styling_carry_over, high voice_adherence, moderate regional_specificity, moderate-high narrative_continuity.
emphasis: Lead with Strength Without Limits (warranty, dependability) and Your Choice Our Power (HEMI, Cummins)
Less weight on Built to Dominate and Fearless by Form
Warmth and celebration register - giving-and-getting holiday close, not category disruption
Holiday lights, year-end iconography acceptable where script directs
year_end_moment.operative_tonal_frame: 'The holidays are all about giving around here / But at the Ram Wrap up the Year Sales event, it's all about getting.'
